text.headline: Marc A. Hesse - Academic Chronicle
text.text: A computational geoscientist's journey from geology to fluid dynamics, porous media, and planetary science.

text.headline: Edinburgh Geological Society Prize
text.text: Awarded the Edinburgh Geological Society Prize during undergraduate studies at the University of Edinburgh.

text.headline: BSc in Geology - University of Edinburgh
text.text: Completed BSc in Geology at the University of Edinburgh, establishing foundation in earth sciences.

text.headline: MS in Oceanography - MIT-WHOI Joint Program
text.text: Master of Science in Oceanography from the MIT-WHOI Joint Program, awarded Presidential Graduate Fellowship.

text.headline: MPhil in Fluid Flow - University of Cambridge
text.text: MPhil in Fluid Flow from Cambridge University's Department of Applied Mathematics and Theoretical Physics, marking pivot toward mathematical modeling.

text.headline: PhD in Petroleum Engineering - Stanford University
text.text: Doctoral degree in Petroleum Engineering from Stanford University, specializing in porous media and fluid dynamics.

text.headline: Postdoctoral Researcher - Brown University
text.text: Postdoctoral researcher in tectonophysics at Brown University, bridging doctoral work and faculty career.

text.headline: Joined UT Austin as Assistant Professor
text.text: Marc Hesse joined the Jackson School of Geosciences and the Institute for Computational Engineering and Sciences (now Oden Institute) as Assistant Professor.

text.headline: Jackson Centennial Teaching Fellowship
text.text: Awarded the John A. and Katherine G. Jackson Centennial Teaching Fellowship in Geological Sciences.

text.headline: Shell Companies Foundation Centennial Chair
text.text: Awarded the Shell Companies Foundation Centennial Chair in Geophysics.

text.headline: Bravo Dome CO2 Research Published
text.text: Published landmark study on Bravo Dome natural CO2 reservoir, estimating 366 MtCO2 dissolved into brine over 1.2-1.4 Ma.

text.headline: DOE Featured Research
text.text: Research on CO2 sequestration featured on the front page of the U.S. Department of Energy website.

text.headline: Dave P. Carlton Centennial Professorship
text.text: Appointed to the Dave P. Carlton Centennial Professorship in Geology, currently serving as Professor in the Department of Earth and Planetary Sciences.

text.headline: Icy Ocean Worlds Research
text.text: Current research focus on habitability of icy moons including Europa and Enceladus, hydrothermal evolution of primordial planetesimals.
